copy.title: Tigers walk off Rays in ten. Brewers demolish Giants. Knicks-Spurs Finals tip tomorrow night.

lead.headline: Brewers demolished Giants 16-2. Complete mismatch.
lead.whatHappened: Milwaukee didn't just beat San Francisco—they embarrassed them. The Brewers put up 16 runs while the Giants managed only 2. This wasn't competitive baseball.
lead.whyBullet1: That kind of offensive explosion usually signals one team is clicking hard while the other is completely lost.
lead.whyBullet2: A 14-run gap tells you everything about where each lineup stands right now.
lead.whatToSay: Yeah, the Brewers just went full destruction mode on the Giants. 16-2 isn't a game, it's a statement.

culture.topic: Sydney Sweeney claps back at Euphoria hate
culture.whatHappened: Sydney Sweeney responded to critics of Euphoria Season 3 with a three-word response that cut through the noise.
culture.whyItMatters: She's tired of people pretending they care about the show's substance when they just want to complain online.
culture.whatToSay: She's not wrong — people spend more time posting about shows they hate than watching stuff they actually like.

culture.topic: Germany rolled Finland 4-0 in friendly tune-up
culture.whatHappened: Germany demolished Finland 4-0 in a football friendly, making a statement before the summer tournament.
culture.whyItMatters: That's the kind of performance that makes opposing coaches sweat in the film room.
culture.whatToSay: Germany looked sharp — they're going to be dangerous when the tournament actually starts.

culture.whatHappened: Seth Rogen plays a film studio exec who genuinely loves movies but runs a company that exists to make sequels and IP.

copy.finalSharpTake: The Brewers demolished the Giants 16-2, which tells you everything about how wide the talent gap is in baseball right now. Meanwhile, tech's split personality hit again—Nvidia crushed it while Tesla tanked—so your portfolio's probably confused. Tomorrow the Knicks and Spurs tip off the Finals, which matters way more than any regular season game today. Just know this: the market's waiting for Fed clarity more than anything else, and baseball's entertainment value depends entirely on whether your team's actually competing.
